title: 100X
subtitle: Prepare • Protect • Produce
fullTitle: 100X: Prepare The Soil, Protect The Seed, Produce More Than Enough
description: A foundational course on preparing your heart to receive God's word and multiply it a hundredfold.

sessions.title: Prepare The Soil
sessions.verse: If you can't understand the meaning of this parable, how will you understand all the other parables?

content.text: Listen! A farmer went out to plant some seed. As he scattered it across his field, some of the seed fell on a footpath, and the birds came and ate it. Other seed fell on shallow soil with underlying rock. The seed sprouted quickly because the soil was shallow. But the plant soon wilted under the hot sun, and since it didn't have deep roots, it died. Other seed fell among thorns that grew up and choked out the tender plants so they produced no grain. Still other seeds fell on fertile soil, and they sprouted, grew, and produced a crop that was thirty, sixty, and even a hundred times as much as had been planted!
Who is the farmer? God's ministers are the farmers scattering seed across the world (field).
What is the seed? The seed is the word of God. (The revelation of Jesus Christ.)
Where is the soil? The soils represent the hearts of people and the condition of their hearts.
The farmer plants seed by taking God's word to others.

You are about to receive a lot of seed. All throughout the discipleship journey you are going to be immersed in conversations and encounters that will break up the hard places, cut back the thorns, and pull out the unhealthy debris that will hinder growth. The best thing you can do is surrender to the process, yield to the experience, and say yes to what God wants to do in your life.

questions: Have you ever felt like the seed of God's word couldn't take root in your life? What was happening in your heart at the time?

questions.question: According to the parable, what does the seed represent?

content.text: As we go through this study, be honest with yourself. What kind of soil are you? This is not intended to bring shame in your life, but to help you realize the condition of your heart and then ask God and His people for help to be transformed into good soil.
Hard Ground (Pathway) — No Entry
The seed that fell on the footpath represents those who hear the message, only to have Satan come at once and take it away.

Some people have hearts that are unable to receive God's word at all. It's like casting seed on concrete. The seed is exposed and is immediately snatched up by Satan. This ground needs to be dug up, turned, tilled and cultivated.
Shallow Ground (Rocky Places) — Surface Level

The seed on the rocky soil represents those who hear the message and immediately receive it with joy. But since they don't have deep roots, they don't last long. They fall away as soon as they have problems or are persecuted for believing God's word.

Some hearts look good on the surface, but they have solid ground a few inches down. They can receive the word with lots of potential, but their environment won't allow long term growth. Without roots a plant will never produce. Without godly community the word of God will not be able to grow.
Cluttered Ground (Uncultivated) — Distracted

The seed that fell among the thorns represents others who hear God's word, but all too quickly the message is crowded out by the worries of this life, the lure of wealth, and the desire for other things, so no fruit is produced.
This person is so consumed by finances, success, concerns, and the lust of the eyes that they have no room for God. Even as the word is being released they are thinking about other things.
Good Soil (Yielded) — Ready to Produce
And the seed that fell on good soil represents those who hear and accept God's word and produce a harvest of thirty, sixty, or even a hundred times as much as had been planted!
This person is like a sponge. They soak up the word, sit in it, squeeze it out on other sponges and come back for more. They have a heart of multiplication.

questions: What kind of soil are you right now?
What would need to change for you to become "good soil"?

questions.question: What chokes out the word in the "thorny ground" heart?
options: Worries, wealth, and desires for other things

content.text: Break it up
Plow up the hard ground of your hearts! Do not waste your good seed among thorns.

Let's be honest, some people have hardened hearts that need conditioning before they are able to receive what is about to be sown. The word says to plow up the hard ground. Plowing is not a feel-good thing. It's hard work, it looks messy and it requires submission.
Soften with water
You take care of the earth and water it, making it rich and fertile... You soften the earth with showers and bless its abundant crops.
The rain of God's love can soften any heart. Before you plant the seed of God's word, drench that person in His love. It is the goodness of God that leads men to change their ways and turn to God.
Pull the stones
What about those who appear as good soil only to have bedrock a few inches below the surface? This soil also needs to be plowed, broken up and cleaned of the stones that will hinder long term growth.
Cutback the overgrowth
Circumcise yourselves to the Lord and remove the foreskins of your hearts.

In farming terms, cutback the areas of your life that are hindering good growth. Surrender doing things your way. Pride and personal power are thorns that will hold you back from producing in God's Kingdom.

questions: What role do you play in the transformation of your own heart?

questions.question: What softens hardened hearts according to this lesson?

content.text: When Isaac planted his crops that year, he harvested a hundred times more grain than he planted, for the Lord blessed him. He became a very rich man, and his wealth continued to grow.
Here's a promise. God wants His word to multiply in you. Because of this promise, we can stand on the truth that there is no soil (no heart condition) that can't be transformed.
You crown the year with a bountiful harvest; even the hard pathways overflow with abundance. The grasslands of the wilderness become a lush pasture, and the hillsides blossom with joy.
Even the hard ground will overflow. Even the wilderness will become lush. Our job is to remain surrendered.
Be still and know that He is God and you are His beloved child. He knows the condition of your heart and what needs to happen so that you might produce 100X.

questions: What areas of your life feel like "wilderness" that you're believing God to make lush?

questions.question: How much did Isaac's crops multiply?

sessions.title: Protect The Seed
sessions.verse: And the peace of God, which surpasses all comprehension, will guard your hearts and minds in Christ Jesus.
warmUp: You've made an investment into a business. What are some ways you would protect your investment?

content.text: You have invested lots of resources into a business. Naturally, you would be upset if someone broke in and stole merchandise or equipment. Well this is no different. God has invested seed in your heart. And to God, you are a garden worth protecting.
The thief's purpose is to steal and kill and destroy. My purpose is to give them a rich and satisfying life.
Don't be naive, we have an enemy who roams around pretending to be a lion and looking for someone to devour. The enemy will come and attack the seed in various ways.
Catch all the foxes, those little foxes, before they ruin the vineyard of love, for the grapevines are blossoming!
The foxes are anything that brings chaos into the garden and destroys the crop.

questions: What are the foxes in your life?
How will you protect the garden of your heart?

questions.question: According to John 10:10, what is the thief's purpose?

content.text: Community • Consistency • Consecration
Community

God has never been alone. Ever! And since you are made in their image you were never meant for isolation. In community, we help remind one another who we are in Christ. We help protect one another from attacks of the enemy. Community is the number one thing that you can do to protect the seed.

Brothers and sisters, even if a person is caught in any wrongdoing, you who are spiritual are to restore such a person in a spirit of gentleness; each one looking to yourself, so that you are not tempted as well. Bear one another's burdens, and thereby fulfill the law of Christ.
Consistency

You have just been immersed in God's word and saturated in His presence. This is not a one time event. It's a way of life. I need His Word in my life everyday. I don't have to read His word, I need to.
Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes out of the mouth of God.
Consecration

This is a powerful word that simply means to be set apart for a Holy purpose. After an ARK Training, it's important that you don't return back to life as usual. Protect your eyes and ears from the media and influences that will not reinforce your Identity through the Trinity.
Therefore, ridding yourselves of all filthiness and all that remains of wickedness, in humility receive the word implanted, which is able to save your souls.

questions: Which of the Three C's is your weakest area?
What practical steps can you take this week to strengthen that area?

questions.question: What are the Three C's of Protection?

content.text: Jesus is the head and we are His body. As the head, He wants to set up a fence of protection. Will you allow Him to set this up for your protection?
1. Surrender to the community — Allow His Body to protect you as God intended.
Be of the same mind toward one another; do not be haughty in mind, but associate with the lowly. Do not be wise in your own estimation.
2. Consume His word consistently — Allow His promises to be your provision.
Now these people were more noble-minded than those in Thessalonica, for they received the word with great eagerness, examining the Scriptures daily to see whether these things were so.
3. Separate yourself from the world — Allow Jesus to be your Priest.
Therefore, if anyone cleanses himself from these things, he will be an implement for honor, sanctified, useful to the Master, prepared for every good work.

questions: What are practical ways you can partner with God in each area?

questions.question: What does consecration mean?
options: To be set apart for a holy purpose

sessions.title: The Three Tests
sessions.verse: In this you greatly rejoice, even though now for a little while, if necessary, you have been distressed by various trials, so that the proof of your faith, being more precious than gold which perishes though tested by fire, may be found to result in praise, glory, and honor at the revelation of Jesus Christ.
warmUp: What is one way that God tests you?

content.text: A good teacher will not test you on something you have not been taught. Good teachers test you to prove that what they taught has become understanding. However, a bad teacher will test you to expose things you do not know.

Dear brothers and sisters, when troubles of any kind come your way, consider it an opportunity for great joy. For you know that when your faith is tested, your endurance has a chance to grow. So let it grow, for when your endurance is fully developed, you will be perfect and complete, needing nothing.
The enemy uses tests to expose and remind you of the old you. But God will use tests to reveal who you truly are.
God blesses those who patiently endure testing and temptation. Afterward they will receive the crown of life that God has promised to those who love him.

These two words keep sticking out: "patiently endure". Cultivation is a process that cannot be rushed. Remember this is about consistently making small acts of obedience that lead up to a life of massive transformation.

questions: Can you think of a time when a test revealed something good about who you are in Christ?

content.text: Immediately after the public declaration at His baptism, Jesus is led by the Holy Spirit into the wilderness to fast. After 40 days, here comes Satan. What does Satan mean? Satan literally means "the accuser."

Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. And after He had fasted for forty days and forty nights, He then became hungry. And the tempter came and said to Him, "If You are the Son of God, command that these stones become bread." But He answered and said, "It is written: 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes out of the mouth of God.'"
The first temptation attacked Jesus' provision — using His power for personal needs.

Then the devil took Him along into the holy city and had Him stand on the pinnacle of the temple, and he said to Him, "If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down; for it is written: 'He will give His angels orders concerning You.'" Jesus said to him, "On the other hand, it is written: 'You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.'"
The second temptation attacked Jesus' identity — proving who He was through spectacle.

Again, the devil took Him along to a very high mountain and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory; and he said to Him, "All these things I will give You, if You fall down and worship me." Then Jesus said to him, "Go away, Satan! For it is written: 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and serve Him only.'"
The third temptation attacked Jesus' allegiance — offering a shortcut to authority.
Each temptation attacks a different part of our being with a lie against an area of life. Every temptation we face as humans falls into one of these three categories.

questions: Which of these three areas (provision, identity, allegiance) does the enemy most often attack in your life?

questions.question: How did Jesus respond to every temptation?

content.text: You are of your father the devil, and you want to do the desires of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth because there is no truth in him. Whenever he tells a lie, he speaks from his own nature, because he is a liar and the father of lies.
Satan comes to Jesus and says... "If you are the Son of God...?" But is this what The Father calls Jesus?
What does the Father call Jesus in Matthew 3 when He was baptized? "This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."
Satan will never, ever call you beloved. He will do whatever it takes to make you feel unloved.

The one thing that Satan does not want you to believe is that you are God's beloved. If we are going to protect the seed of Christ in us, the number one thing we can do is remain unshakable in our identity as God's beloved children.
You may feel unloved. You may feel a lot of things throughout this life but we walk by faith and not by sight. By faith I accept my God given identity as a beloved child of God.
Declaration: "I am God's beloved!"

questions: When the enemy comes to test your seed, will you try to prove yourself to others? Or stand on the identity that God has declared over your life?

questions.question: What word did Satan leave out when tempting Jesus?

sessions.title: Produce 100X
sessions.verse: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
warmUp: What fruit of the Holy Spirit are you producing the most of?

lessons.title: A Tree of Its Own Kind

content.text: The earth produced vegetation, plants yielding seed according to their kind, and trees bearing fruit with seed in them, according to their kind; and God saw that it was good.

You have the seed of Christ growing on the inside of you. This seed will produce fruit and in that fruit is more seed. Fruit is meant to be enjoyed by others and the seed is meant to be replanted to produce even more.
Multiplication is the natural result of a heart that has been purified into good soil.
You will know them by their fruits. Grapes are not gathered from thorn bushes, nor figs from thistles, are they? So every good tree bears good fruit, but the bad tree bears bad fruit.
Understand this: a seed will always produce after its own kind. If you sow the seed of Christ, you get more of Christ in the people around you.
For as the earth produces its sprouts, And as a garden causes the things sown in it to spring up, So the Lord God will cause righteousness and praise to spring up before all the nations.

questions: What kind of fruit is your life producing right now?

questions.question: What does a seed always produce?

content.text: I am the vine, you are the branches; the one who remains in Me, and I in him bears much fruit, for apart from Me you can do nothing... My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit, and so prove to be My disciples.
The proof of being His disciple is that you bear much fruit — not some fruit, but a lot of fruit.

I planted the seed in your hearts, and Apollos watered it, but it was God who made it grow. It's not important who does the planting, or who does the watering. What's important is that God makes the seed grow.

After the seed of Christ has been sown into someone's life, you may water that seed. But you also may not. Someone else may be called to water and cultivate the seed of Christ that came from your life. We are all working together for the same harvest.
Exponential Discipleship vs. Conversion Growth
Would you rather have 10,000 disciples every year for 30 years (300,000 total)? Or disciples that double every year for 30 years (over 5 million)?
Truly, truly I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.

questions: Are you currently investing in anyone's spiritual growth?
Who has God placed in your life that you could begin discipling?

questions.question: What proves we are Jesus' disciples according to John 15?

content.text: One person gives freely, yet gains even more; another withholds unduly, but comes to poverty. A generous person will prosper; whoever refreshes others will be refreshed.
Receiving + Giving = Growing
Radical receivers become radical givers. Radical givers experience radical growth.

Giving is like working out. What makes your muscles sore when you first start working out is not the same weight that will make you sore 6 months later. Giving should not hurt so much that it puts you in the hospital. It should hurt like sore muscles the day after you workout.
Now He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
Prayer: "Father, thank you for providing my needs and funding my dreams. How would you like me to be a radical receiver and a faithful giver this season?"

questions: What does it look like to give radically in these three areas: Time, Talent, and Treasure?
When you think about being radical in your receiving and giving, what feelings or responses are you having?

questions.question: According to Proverbs 11, what happens to a generous person?
